programme.title: TikTok Star Murder Trial
programme.desc: The toxic marriage fueled by constant domestic violence, emotional manipulation and verbal abuse eventually exploded into double homicide. But instead of a murder plot carried out by a jealous, controlling husband, Ali Abulaban's defense claimed the murders occurred out of a cocaine-binged heat of passion. In the Defense's closing arguments, Ali's attorney claimed that he was growing more mental unstable as his fame was rising on social media to the point that he became a raging coke addict.

programme.title: TikTok Star Murder Trial
programme.desc: The Defense walks the jury through the day Ana Abulaban and her friend, Rayburn Barron, were shot.

programme.title: TikTok Star Murder Trial
programme.desc: The lead prosecutor buttoned up their case against Ali Abulaban in the State's rebuttal. Taren Brast reminded the jury of the defendant's demeanor when she was drilling him under cross-examination. This was to highlight the anger and need for control which prompted Ali to kill his wife, Ana, and her friend, Rayburn Barron. Next, jurors would deliberate over Ali's role and fate in this fatal ambush apartment attack where two young lives were tragically lost.

programme.title: TikTok Star Murder Trial
programme.desc: Since there was no dispute that Ali Abulaban killed the two victims, jurors were instead tasked with deciding whether the killings were first or second-degree murder or voluntary manslaughter. Before the sentencing of Ali Abulaban, his defense appealed his murder conviction. Ali faced life in prison without the possibility of parole for being convicted of two counts of first-degree murder in the shooting deaths of his wife, Ana, and her friend, Rayburn Barron.

programme.title: TikTok Star Murder Trial
programme.desc: Judge Jeffrey Fraser listened to the emotional victim-impact statements ahead of his sentencing of Ali Abulaban, the convicted double murderer. The relatives of Ana and her friend, Rayburn Barron, talked about how their murders destroyed each of their families. Ali then addressed the Court. He told the judge that a life in prison without parole is like being condemned to hell.

programme.title: Bloodline Detectives hosted by Nancy Grace

programme.title: Bloodline Detectives hosted by Nancy Grace
programme.desc: In 1991, 16-year-old Sarah Yarborough is murdered outside her school. Eyewitnesses spot a man, but the case remains cold for nearly 30 years - until forensic genealogy unmasks the killer.

programme.title: Bloodline Detectives hosted by Nancy Grace
programme.desc: Anna Hlavka, 20, is raped and murdered in Portland in 1979. Her case goes cold - until DNA and forensic genealogy link it to a serial killer.

programme.title: Bloodline Detectives hosted by Nancy Grace
programme.desc: In July 1967, 20-year-old Susan Galvin is found dead in a parking garage elevator at Seattle Center. After over 50 years, detectives reopen the investigation with the help of forensic genetic genealogy in 2018.

programme.title: Bloodline Detectives hosted by Nancy Grace
programme.desc: In October 2003, the San Diego County community is shaken by a gruesome discovery: the dismembered legs of a woman are found in a dumpster at the Rancho San Diego apartment complex. The case goes cold until DNA technology finds the killer.

programme.title: Bloodline Detectives hosted by Nancy Grace
programme.desc: In 1985, Terri McAdams is murdered in her fiance's apartment. In 2023, DNA and genealogy identify the killer.

programme.title: Bloodline Detectives hosted by Nancy Grace
programme.desc: In 1974, 25-year-old Mary K. Schlais is murdered while hitchhiking from Minneapolis to Madison, Wisconsin.

programme.title: Bloodline Detectives hosted by Nancy Grace
programme.desc: In 1976, 16-year-old Pamela Maurer leaves a friend's house to walk to a nearby location for a soft drink. She never makes it home. The next morning, road workers discover her body along College Road. New DNA technology solves the case in 2019.

programme.title: Bloodline Detectives hosted by Nancy Grace
programme.desc: A 65-year-old mystery is solved when DNA testing identifies the remains of 7-year-old Chester Alfred Breiney.

programme.title: Bloodline Detectives hosted by Nancy Grace
programme.desc: In 1982, 5-year-old Anne Pham vanishes on her way to kindergarten. Despite the FBI, the military police and the Seaside Police Department working together, the investigation goes cold until genealogy identifies her killer.

programme.title: Bloodline Detectives hosted by Nancy Grace
programme.sub-title: The Hair That Called His Bluff
programme.desc: A single strand of hair, a DNA match and decades of detective work finally solve the murder of schoolgirl Rashell Ward after over 40 years.

programme.title: Bloodline Detectives hosted by Nancy Grace
programme.desc: Mark Dribin vanishes from Portland in 1999. After over 20 years, cutting-edge DNA technology finally unmasks his killer - and unearths even darker secrets.

programme.title: Bloodline Detectives hosted by Nancy Grace
programme.desc: In 2013, a newborn baby know as "Baby Precious" is found dead. DNA technology tracks down the child's parents.

programme.title: Bloodline Detectives hosted by Nancy Grace

programme.title: Bloodline Detectives hosted by Nancy Grace

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: In 1988, a criminal confessed that two community members hired him to murder a South Carolina farmer.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: Vince Gigante, the reputed boss of the Genovese crime family, wandered the streets of New York wearing only pajamas and a bathrobe, but the FBI was convinced he was anything but crazy.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: On October 1, 1993, a stranger abducted 12-year-old Polly Klaas during a slumber party in her home. FBI agents collected fibers from her bedroom and discovered, through the use of laser light sources, a palm print on Polly's bed.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: In 1997, Jamie McMahan and Christopher Kauffman went on a crime spree of robbery, kidnapping and murder.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: In 1993, a mother, her 8-year-old son and a live-in nurse were brutally murdered by an assassin. The murder-for-hire plot was meticulously planned and executed.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: In 1996, a group of political extremists robbed a bank in Washington using homemade bombs and military precision to escape. The FBI learned that the robbers might be preparing for a small-scale war with law enforcement.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: A special task force was formed in the early 1990s to combat the flow of Colombia cocaine into New York. As part of an elaborate sting operation, one detective went undercover and infiltrated a drug gang connected to Pablo Escobar's Medellin cartel.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: In rural Texas, a millionaire's teenage daughter was kidnapped on her way to school. Local authorities and the FBI conducted a massive search for the girl while her father attempted to deliver the ransom cash to a drop the kidnappers kept changing.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: No suspect had emerged after the rapes and murders of ten women near Tampa, Florida in 1984. The killer was a ruthless and terrifying predator, the newest member of an infamous group known as "serial killers."

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: In Boston, FBI agents tracked down a gang of robbers who specialized in armored car takedowns. As the robberies grew increasingly more violent, authorities launched a complex sting to trap the deadly ring of thieves.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: The decapitation of an African-American male led the Miami-Dade police and the FBI along a trail of murder.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: In June 1973, a young girl was abducted from a state park while camping with her family. A criminal profiler predicted that the kidnapper would contact the girl's family on the one-year anniversary of her abduction.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: In 1990, a 14-year-old girl ran away from her Texas home and disappeared; six months later, her body was found hundreds of miles away in an Illinois barn. Agents tracked down a trucker who they believed was responsible for over 50 murders.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: In the late 1980s, an illegal drug operation known as the K Street Crew opened its doors in Washington, DC.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: After two brothers abduct and murder bank president Dan Short, FBI agents slowly piece together tiny shreds of evidence. Eventually, they're able to convict the Agofsky brothers for the 1989 Noel, Missouri bank robbery.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: In July 1997, three people were shot to death at a Georgetown coffee shop. Investigators could find no immediate suspect or no clear motive for the murders. Was this a hate crime, a robbery gone wrong or an act of cold-blooded vengeance?

programme.title: World's Most Evil Killers
programme.desc: She became the first woman to be executed by lethal injection after taking six lives. What drove her to commit these callous murders?

programme.title: World's Most Evil Killers
programme.desc: A dangerous sexual predator murders a young woman, just days after his release from prison.

programme.title: World's Most Evil Killers
programme.desc: An investigation into the man known as the 'Babes in the Wood Killer,' who evaded justice for thirty years.

programme.title: World's Most Evil Killers
programme.desc: Robert Hansen was a monster who preyed on the most vulnerable women in society - dehumanising them for his own twisted fantasies. Stranded with their killer in the vast Alaskan wilderness his victims had no chance of survival.

programme.title: World's Most Evil Killers
programme.desc: Mary's murder haunted Glasgow for 36 years with no witnesses or leads until advanced forensic techniques finally revealed Graham as the killer, ending decades of uncertainty for her family and investigators.

programme.title: World's Most Evil Killers
programme.desc: A violent spree left three innocent people dead as Anthony robbed, fled and attacked without hesitation. Police launched a massive manhunt, racing to catch him before he could claim yet another victim.

programme.title: World's Most Evil Killers
programme.desc: In 2007, single mother of three Paige Birgfeld went missing. Police in Grand Junction, Colorado investigating her disappearance discovered she'd been secretly working as an escort and suspected one of her clients of abducting and killing her.

programme.title: World's Most Evil Killers
programme.desc: Over six years, Eddie Seda, the copycat Zodiac killer stalked the streets of New York City, in search of easy targets. His cowardly attacks culminated with the ferocious murder of a woman, who had only asked him for a cigarette.

programme.title: World's Most Evil Killers
programme.desc: Steven Dean Gordon and Frank Cano murdered at least four sex workers in California between 2012 and 2013.

programme.title: World's Most Evil Killers
programme.desc: The story of a terrifying killer who snatched away the lives of four victims before being caught while trying to claim his fifth.

programme.title: World's Most Evil Killers
programme.desc: A routine traffic stop revealed a body in Joel's car, unraveling a long string of murders targeting vulnerable women. The discovery ended his violent reign and gave investigators answers long out of reach.

programme.title: World's Most Evil Killers
programme.desc: Keith Jesperson, known as the Smiley Face Killer, killed eight women across several states in the 1990s.

programme.title: World's Most Evil Killers
programme.desc: He portrayed himself as a pillar of the community, but the story of this cult killer was deeply sinister.

programme.title: World's Most Evil Killers
programme.desc: The story of a killer so evil that not even her own son could escape her clutches. She became the first woman to be executed in Florida since 1848.

programme.title: World's Most Evil Killers
programme.desc: A father took his children out for food after murdering his wife, then strangled them when they returned home.

programme.title: World's Most Evil Prisoners
programme.desc: Being behind bars only made Italian-American mobster Freddy Geas even more brutal.

programme.title: World's Most Evil Prisoners
programme.desc: At 18, Christa Pike commits a brutal murder and becomes one of the youngest ever women sent to death row.

programme.title: World's Most Evil Prisoners
programme.desc: Gary Vinter, in prison for double murder, stabs a fellow inmate in the eye and gives another brain damage.

programme.title: World's Most Evil Prisoners
programme.desc: Nothing was going to stop Ricky Dubose from breaking free of prison. Whilst being transported on a bus between facilities, he killed two correctional officers before escaping - and going on the run.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: A car carrying three men pulls up alongside another on an Alaskan highway and fires shots, leaving a passenger dead. One of the passengers in the killer's car agrees to testify against his friends. The resulting trials don't end the carnage.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: A college student was found dead, and the evidence suggested he knew his killer. Three hairs and some microscopic cells helped police to unravel a web of lies, and find the motive for murder.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: In an affluent suburb, police were called to the scene of what appeared to be an accidental drowning.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: When two women from the same town were murdered in the same way, police feared a serial killer was on the loose. At first they thought the victims had nothing in common until they found tiny clues linking them to the same man.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: A woman was found dead on a golf course. The grass on the course was so distinctive, it had evidentiary value.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: A teenager went missing after an evening of horseback riding. Her body was found a month later, three miles from her home. The killer unknowingly left trace evidence behind - tiny but unmistakable clues which pointed to him and him alone.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: When a popular disc jockey was found murdered in a community garden, police swung into action. A sniffer dog and a blood spatter expert led police to the killer and he'd been much closer than they realized.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: When a man is shot down in his garage, police discover that a wound on the widow may have been self-inflicted.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: Three seemingly unrelated deaths proved to be serial murders. The killer used poison which had no taste.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: When a victim is brutally murdered in his bed, investigators find a shoe impression in the mud outside.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: Police hoped the shoe impressions found at a crime scene would put their investigation back on track.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: When the head chef of a historic Philadelphia restaurant was found dead, investigators interviewed the usual suspects: family, friends and coworkers. As they sifted through the evidence, police uncovered a chilling tale of debt and deceit.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: A serial arsonist was on the loose. It was up to forensic scientists to find the clues that lay in the ashes.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: When Genell Plude is found dead in her bathroom, the scene points to suicide. But a coroner's inquest and a unique application of forensic science gave investigators a different explanation for her death.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: Three homicides on two continents looked like professional executions. Investigators on both sides of the Atlantic needed to find out if they were related and, if they were, who or what they had in common.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: A 29-year-old woman was killed instantly when a bomb exploded in her home. The device was so powerful that shrapnel was embedded in houses across the street. The bomber had not only knowledge and skill, but also a motive for murder.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: A killer tried to incinerate and destroy everything that could link him to his crime. But in doing so, he inadvertently created new forensic evidence, evidence which came to light with a technique never before used in a criminal investigation.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: A woman appears to have lost control of her SUV and plunged down an embankment into a shallow creek. But investigators suspect foul play when they find snowy footprints leading from the crime scene.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: A Russian immigrant is found dead and a strange phone call to his office is the only lead investigators have.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: The prime suspect had a criminal record and his driver's license was found at the scene of a brutal double homicide.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: When a little girl got sick and died, investigators were stumped. Was it an accident, an unexplained illness or murder?

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: When a car was found in a drainage ditch with two bodies inside, a fingertip torn from a latex glove would point investigators to both the crime scene and the killer.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: An investigation into the murder of a World War II veteran slowed to a halt when the prime suspect had a solid alibi. But a lucky break led to a shady character who wore distinctive boots.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: A woman who was known to have suffered from depression took her own life. But her sister told police that she said if anything were to happen to her there would be a note in the china cabinet.

programme.title: Forensic Files

programme.title: Forensic Files

programme.desc: A driver said he couldn't have hit and killed a pedestrian because his Jeep had been sold months ago.

programme.desc: A man was killed in a car crash, but the evidence led investigators to believe it was not an accident.
